Implement complete WebCraft browser-based Minecraft clone

- Procedural terrain generation with simplex-noise (biomes: plains, forest, desert)
- 27 block types with programmatic canvas texture atlas (256×256)
- Per-face chunk mesher with solid/water/plant geometry separation
- AABB physics with separate X/Y/Z collision resolution
- Player movement: walk, sprint, sneak, jump, swim, fly mode
- Pointer-lock first-person camera with yaw/pitch
- Block breaking (hardness-based) and placement via raycasting
- Full drag-and-drop inventory (36 slots) and hotbar
- Pattern-matching crafting system with 15+ recipes
- Day/night sky cycle with dynamic lighting and fog
- IndexedDB world persistence via localforage
- Title screen, loading progress bar, pause menu with settings
- Mobile virtual joystick and touch controls
- Debug overlay (F3): FPS, position, chunk, biome info
- Complete Minecraft-style CSS with responsive design

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix middleware execution by using production mode for E2E tests

- Updated Playwright config to use production build instead of dev mode
- Next.js 16 Turbopack has known issues with middleware execution in dev mode
- Middleware works correctly in production, confirmed by passing tests
- Added documentation explaining the issue and solution
- All route protection tests now pass (17/19 navigation tests pass)
- Build passes, all 257 unit tests pass

Co-authored-by: simongottipalli <8577369+simongottipalli@users.noreply.github.com>

* Delete docs/MIDDLEWARE-DEV-MODE-ISSUE.md

* Fix CI E2E tests by building before starting production server

- Updated Playwright config to run build in CI before starting production server
- Increased timeout to 180s to account for build time
- Local development still reuses existing build for faster iteration
- Fixes "Could not find a production build in the '.next' directory" error

Co-authored-by: simongottipalli <8577369+simongottipalli@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix E2E mobile navigation tests by waiting for links to be visible

- Add explicit wait for mobile navigation links to be visible before clicking
- Fixes timeout issues in CI where React hydration may be slower in production mode
- Ensures mobile menu is fully rendered before interacting with links

Co-authored-by: simongottipalli <8577369+simongottipalli@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix mobile navigation E2E tests by waiting for authentication

- Wait for user greeting to appear before checking for navigation links
- Ensures AuthProvider has completed user session fetch
- Fixes timing issue where mobile menu renders before user data loads
- Scopes link queries within mobile-nav container for better reliability

feat(observability): instrument core orchestration paths with OTel spans

Add OpenTelemetry spans to all 9 core orchestration services per §10.13.2:
- task.transition in TransitionService
- task.assign in SchedulerService
- worker.prepare/worker.run in WorkerSupervisorService
- worker.heartbeat in HeartbeatService
- validation.run in ValidationRunnerService
- review.route in ReviewRouterService
- review.lead_decision in ReviewDecisionService
- merge.prepare/merge.execute in MergeExecutorService

Create span name and attribute key constants in @factory/observability.
Add 14 span verification tests. Re-export SpanStatusCode, Span, and
InMemorySpanExporter from the observability package.

commit.message:
feat(backend-ts): wire GitHub OAuth auth routes

- Flip /api/auth/enabled from hardcoded false to config.authEnabled
- Add 5 auth routes: /auth/github (redirect), /auth/callback (exchange),
  /api/auth/me (get user), /auth/logout, /auth/refresh
- JWT in httpOnly cookie (trex_token), 24h expiry
- OAuth state in httpOnly cookie for CSRF protection
- @fastify/cookie for cookie parse/set

Set TREX_AUTH_ENABLED=true + TREX_GITHUB_CLIENT_ID + SECRET to enable.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: trex

commit.message:
feat(control-plane): implement artifact and review packet retrieval endpoints

Add REST endpoints for retrieving task artifacts, review history,
review cycle packets, parsed packet content, and merge details.

Endpoints:
- GET /tasks/:taskId/artifacts - artifact tree organized by type
- GET /tasks/:taskId/packets/:packetId - parsed JSON packet content
- GET /tasks/:taskId/reviews - review cycle history with decisions
- GET /tasks/:taskId/reviews/:cycleId/packets - specialist packets
- GET /tasks/:taskId/merge - merge queue item + validation runs

New files:
- review/artifacts.controller.ts + artifacts.service.ts
- review/reviews.controller.ts + reviews.service.ts
- merge/merge-details.controller.ts + merge-details.service.ts
- 6 test files with 31 new tests (3,268 total passing)

Closes T084.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(frontend): center collapsed sidebar button — p-0, justify-center, gap-0

Replace p-2 with p-0 + justify-center + gap-0 when collapsed so the
icon centers in the 32px button without padding/gap offset. Circle
widget uses inset-0 to fill the button and center via flexbox.
Close button hidden when collapsed.

commit.message:
Fix profile workdir: use local repo for Copilot boot, skip git ops

Copilot needs a repo with init.ps1 to emit 'Environment loaded:' —
using TEMP caused a 30min boot timeout. Now uses the local repo path
without any git fetch/checkout.

commit.message:
feat(randutil): add random value generation utilities (#607)

New package with 11 random utility functions:
- Int, Float64, Bool: basic random values
- Pick[T]: random element from slice
- Shuffle[T]: random permutation
- Sample[T]: random subset without replacement
- String, Hex, Alpha, AlphaNum: random string generators
- WeightedPick[T]: weighted random selection

All generic functions use math/rand/v2 for performance.
Includes doc.go, 25+ unit tests, 3 benchmarks, 3 examples.

Co-authored-by: Jordan <jordan@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(application): implement audit event query service (T074)

Add AuditQueryService with getEntityTimeline() and searchAuditEvents()
for querying the append-only audit log with flexible, combinable filters
and offset-based pagination.

- New AuditEventQueryPort (read-side, CQRS-lite separation from write port)
- AuditEventFilters: entityType, entityId, eventType, actorType, actorId, startTime, endTime
- PaginatedAuditResult with total count and page metadata
- Input validation (non-empty entity params, time range consistency)
- Pagination normalization (clamping, defaults)
- Infrastructure: findByEntityPaginated() and searchFiltered() with Drizzle dynamic queries
- Infrastructure: createAuditEventQueryPortAdapter() with shared mapRowToRecord()
- 26 unit tests covering timelines, filters, pagination, validation, edge cases

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

test(discovery): add fuzz tests for registry operations (#606)

Add four fuzz targets:
- FuzzRegister: arbitrary service/ID/addr with sentinel error verification
- FuzzResolve: resolution with arbitrary service names
- FuzzSetStatus: status transitions with arbitrary inputs
- FuzzHeartbeat: heartbeat with arbitrary service/ID pairs

commit.message:
feat: implement Azure Incident Copilot MVP

Complete TypeScript monorepo with all 87 tasks across 9 phases:

- Shared package: types, validation, SQLite persistence (node:sqlite),
  log/job/result repositories, mock AI analysis adapter, seed data
- API server: Express REST API with incident CRUD, log submission,
  job tracking, analysis results, correlation IDs, CORS
- Worker: polling-based async processor with mock AI analysis
- Frontend: React + Vite with incident list, create, detail pages,
  log submission, analysis display, job status polling
- Tests: 67 tests (unit + API integration + E2E workflow)
- Scripts: dev.sh (concurrent startup), verify.sh (E2E verification)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(cond): add generic conditional and comparison utilities (#513)

New package with 10 generic utility functions:
- Ternary[T]: inline conditional expression
- Coalesce[T], CoalesceFunc[T]: first non-zero value
- Clamp[T]: restrict to range
- Min[T], Max[T]: variadic min/max
- Zero[T], IsZero[T]: zero value helpers
- Switch[T]: pattern matching with Case structs

All functions are generic and type-safe.
Includes doc.go, 25+ unit tests, 3 benchmarks, 4 examples.

commit.message:
feat(encoding): add base62 encode/decode utilities (#506)

Add a new encoding package with base62 encoding for compact, URL-safe
representations of binary data and integers.

New functions:
- Base62Encode — encode bytes to base62 string
- Base62Decode — decode base62 string to bytes
- Base62EncodeUint64 — encode uint64 to compact base62
- Base62DecodeUint64 — decode base62 string to uint64

Includes 14 tests, 2 benchmarks, and 2 fuzz targets.
100% statement coverage.

Co-authored-by: Jordan <jordan@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(application): implement rework loop with rejection context (T062)

Add ReworkContextService that assembles a validated RejectionContext from
persisted review data (LeadReviewDecision + specialist ReviewPackets +
DevResultPacket) for inclusion in rework TaskPackets.

New files:
- ports/rework-context.ports.ts — Port interfaces for querying rejected
  review data
- services/rework-context.service.ts — Service that builds RejectionContext
  at query time from already-persisted data
- services/rework-context.service.test.ts — 22 tests covering happy path,
  fallbacks, error handling, and defensive parsing

Design: RejectionContext is derived from existing records rather than stored
redundantly, keeping data normalized and avoiding schema migrations.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: refresh business roadmap reality check

- update roadmap with current-head Playwright workflow evidence
- refresh Playwright blocker counts and stale documentation notes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(convert): add safe type conversion utilities (#502)

New package with 13 conversion functions:
- ToInt, ToInt64, ToFloat64, ToUint: string-to-number with fallback
- ToBool: flexible boolean parsing (true/1/yes/on)
- ToString: any-to-string conversion
- ToDuration: string-to-Duration with fallback
- MustInt, MustFloat64: panic-on-error conversions
- Ptr[T]: create pointer to value
- Deref[T], DerefOr[T]: safe pointer dereferencing

All functions return sensible defaults instead of errors.
Includes doc.go, 25+ unit tests, 3 benchmarks, 4 examples.

Co-authored-by: Jordan <jordan@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(stringutil): add string utility functions (#496)

New package with 8 string utilities:
- ToKebabCase, ToPascalCase: case conversion
- PadLeft, PadRight: string padding
- IsBlank: empty/whitespace check
- Reverse: rune-aware string reversal
- WordWrap: line wrapping at word boundaries
- CountWords: word counting

All functions are Unicode-aware (rune-based, not byte-based).
Includes doc.go, 20+ unit tests, 3 benchmarks, 1 fuzz, 3+ examples.

Co-authored-by: Jordan <jordan@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(maputil): add generic map utility functions (#487)

New package with 9 generic map utilities:
- Keys, Values: extract keys/values as slices
- Merge: combine multiple maps (last-write-wins)
- Pick, Omit: select/exclude specific keys
- Filter: predicate-based filtering
- MapValues: transform values preserving keys
- Invert: swap keys and values
- Equal: deep equality check

All functions are nil-safe and non-mutating.
Includes doc.go, 22 unit tests, 3 benchmarks, 4 examples.
README updated with package entry.

Co-authored-by: Jordan <jordan@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Refactor profile to use Copilot MCP, skip git, target 100+ comments

- profile command now submits a worker task (like review/revise)
- Worker skips repo init and branch checkout for profile tasks (no git needed)
- Agent uses ADO MCP tools to fetch PRs/threads (no PAT required)
- Prompt instructs agent to collect 100+ comments before building profile
- Removed Build-ReviewerProfile and Build-ProfileFromComments from profile.ps1
- Kept Get/Save-ReviewerProfile for review command usage
- --show flag still works locally (reads from blob storage)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(pubsub): add typed in-process publish/subscribe event bus (#451)

New generic package for decoupled in-process event delivery:
- Bus[T] with type-safe Subscribe, Unsubscribe, Publish, PublishAsync
- Publish is synchronous; PublishAsync delivers in separate goroutines
- Panic recovery with slog error logging
- Concurrent-safe (RWMutex-protected subscription map)
- Context propagation to handlers

Includes doc.go, 11 unit tests, 3 benchmarks, 2 examples.
README updated with package entry.

Co-authored-by: Jordan <jordan@users.noreply.github.com>

  0→100 in sync with the ring arc lerp animation on page load

  data change

commit.message:
fix(frontend): progress rings clockwise direction + animation on refresh

- Arc direction: use atan(x, y) directly — gives 0=top, 0.25=right,
  0.5=bottom, 0.75=left (naturally clockwise). Previous -y negate
  was inverting direction.
- Animation: rings animate even if main post-process shader disabled.
  Moved progressRingsEnabled check outside the config.enabled gate
  so lerp and redraws continue after page refresh.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add sarma review and profile commands

- New lib/profile.ps1: reviewer profile generation from ADO PR history
  - Fetches PRs where reviewer was active, extracts code comments
  - Categorizes by focus area (dead code, edge cases, logging, tests, etc.)
  - Generates structured profile with examples and style analysis
  - Stores/retrieves profiles from Azure Blob Storage

- sarma profile <alias>: build a reviewer's style profile
  - Fetches up to 30 recent PRs, extracts code review comments
  - Auto-categorizes and generates review persona document
  - --show: display existing profile
  - --refresh: regenerate profile

- sarma review <PR> [--alias <reviewer>]: review a PR
  - Without --alias: generic code review
  - With --alias: loads reviewer profile, reviews as that persona
  - Creates comment threads on the PR via ADO MCP

- Worker updated: handles 'review' task type (no branch, copilot-driven)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(vscode): add .NET 10 support for custom code projects (#8895)

* feat(vscode): add .NET 10 support for custom code projects

Add .NET 10 as a selectable target framework across the VS Code extension
and webview for custom code project creation, detection, and debugging.

- Add TargetFramework.Net10 enum value and DotnetVersion.net10 constant
- Create FunctionsFileNet10 and FunctionsProjNet10New template files
- Add net10.0 backup templates for dotnet template fallback
- Expose .NET 10 in extension quick-pick and webview dropdown selectors
- Add DOTNET_10 and DOTNET_10_DESCRIPTION localization strings
- Wire Net10 into template mappings in functionAppFilesStep and
  CreateFunctionAppFiles with shared usesPublishFolderProperty helper
- Extract getCustomCodeRuntime helper for coreclr/clr runtime selection
- Add isCustomCodeNet10Csproj detection and getCustomCodeTargetFramework
  dispatcher in customCodeUtils with usesLogicAppFolderToPublish helper
- Reprioritize .NET version detection to prefer 10 over 8
- Update codeful workflow creation to default to .NET 10
- Add DotnetVersion.net10 to SDK version mapping in updateBuildFile
- Add unit tests for debug config, custom code detection, VSCode contents
  generation, framework version probing, and review step rendering

* feat(vscode): add Program.cs generation for .NET 10 isolated worker model

- Create ProgramFileNet10 template with namespace placeholder
- Update CreateFunctionAppFiles and FunctionAppFilesStep to generate
  Program.cs for .NET 10 custom code projects
- Add 8 tests covering Program.cs creation, namespace replacement,
  and skipping for Net8/NetFx/rulesEngine projects

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(vscode): extract shared function project file utilities

- Extract duplicated template maps and file-creation methods into
  shared functionProjectFiles.ts utility module
- Replace hardcoded framework strings with TargetFramework constants
  in dotNetFrameworkStep and reviewCreateStep
- Remove brittle (step as any) casts in tests

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(vscode): add newline delimiter between version sources in getFramework

Version sources were concatenated with no delimiter, causing the
multiline regex to miss versions when outputs lacked trailing newlines.
For example, '8.0.100' + '10.0.200 [/sdk]' became '8.0.10010.0.200'
which matched neither version.

Using join('\n') ensures each source starts on its own line so the

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: cold-start fallback + include current season in full scrape

- fetch_season_since(): when no base CSV exists, fall back to full
  get_games_season() instead of silently writing only the lookback window.
  This fixes the backfill failure where games_2026.csv was missing.
- seasons_to_fetch(): include current year (2026) so full_rescrape also
  scrapes the in-progress season, not just completed ones (2021-2025).

perf: use --lookback-days 14 for incremental scrape instead of hardcoded date

Rolling 14-day window captures conference tournament games (this week) and
NCAA tournament rounds without re-scraping the full regular season from November.
Replaces the hardcoded 2026-03-15 Selection Sunday date which missed conf tourney.

commit.message:
perf: incremental scrape via --since flag (Selection Sunday onward)

Normal CI runs now use get_games_range(2026-03-15, today) instead of
get_games_season(2026), reducing scrape time from ~3 min to ~5 sec.
New games are merged/deduped into the existing games_2026.csv.

- scrape_with_cbbpy.py: add --since YYYY-MM-DD flag and fetch_season_since()
  using cbbpy.get_games_range(); incremental path deduplicates by game_id
- run_pipeline.py: add --since arg, pass through to run_scrape()
- update-data.yml: scheduled runs now use --since 2026-03-15 (Selection Sunday);
  full_rescrape still re-fetches everything with --historical

commit.message:
Add minimum heart rate and explicit labels to lap analysis

Enhanced analyze_activity lap display:
- Calculate min/max HR from stream data when lap data doesn't provide it
- Heart rate now shows: min:X/avg:Y/max:Z bpm with explicit labels
- Handles cases where Strava lap data lacks min_heartrate field
- Falls back to stream-based calculation for complete HR analysis

Example output:
  Before: ❤️ 150/165 bpm
  After:  ❤️ min:140/avg:150/max:165 bpm

Minimum HR is useful for interval analysis to see recovery
depth between efforts. Stream-based calculation ensures data
is available even for older activities.

All 44 tests passing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: train-with-gpt
repository.description: Get training suggestions based on your data!

commit.message:
Add recovery metrics guidance to start_consultation

Updated consultation guidance to include new data sources:

Recovery Metrics section added:
- get_sleep_data: Sleep duration, quality, stages
- get_hrv_data: HRV with rolling averages (7/14/28 day)
- get_resting_heart_rate: Daily RHR trends

Guidance on when to check recovery data:
- Planning training load increases
- Athlete mentions fatigue or poor performance
- Validating subjective feelings with objective metrics
- Evaluating recovery adequacy

LLM now knows these tools exist and when to use them for
holistic training analysis combining workload and recovery.

commit.message:
Add rolling averages to HRV data (7, 14, 28 days)

Enhanced HRV summary to show:
- Period average for the requested date range
- 7-day rolling average (if ≥7 days of data)
- 14-day rolling average (if ≥14 days of data)
- 28-day rolling average (if ≥28 days of data)

This allows tracking HRV trends over different time windows.
Added test for rolling averages calculation.
All 44 tests passing.

repository.description: Get training suggestions based on your data!

commit.message:
Fix HRV data parsing - data is nested in hrvSummary

The Garmin API returns HRV data nested under 'hrvSummary' key, not at
the top level. Updated tool to parse correctly:
- lastNightAvg is in hrv_data['hrvSummary']['lastNightAvg']
- weeklyAvg is in hrv_data['hrvSummary']['weeklyAvg']
- status is in hrv_data['hrvSummary']['status']

Updated tests to match actual API structure.
All 43 tests passing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: train-with-gpt
repository.description: Get training suggestions based on your data!

commit.message:
Add HRV and resting heart rate tools

Added two new recovery metrics tools:
- get_hrv_data: Heart Rate Variability data with status and trends
- get_resting_heart_rate: Daily resting heart rate tracking

Both tools:
- Support date ranges (up to 30 days)
- Auto-connect to Garmin if needed
- Provide summary statistics
- Follow same pattern as sleep data tool

Added methods to GarminClient:
- get_hrv_data(date)
- get_heart_rates(date)

Includes comprehensive integration tests (12 new tests).
All 43 tests passing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: GameVault — MudBlazor + Blazor MAUI Hybrid game catalog

3-project solution for exploring MudBlazor + MAUI Hybrid from a React background.

Projects:
- GameVault.Shared: Razor Class Library with all pages, components, models, services
- GameVault.Web: Blazor Web App host
- GameVault.Maui: MAUI Blazor Hybrid desktop host (Windows)

Features:
- Dashboard with stat cards, genre/platform charts
- Game Catalog with search, filter, grid/list toggle
- Game Detail page with chips, rating, expandable sections
- 30 mock games via MockGameService
- Dark/light theme toggle
- Shared components: CatalogFilterPanel, GameCard, GameGrid, GameListView

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
